mission.title: argo-workflows: Successfully finished pods show stuck in pending phase indefinitely in GKE + Kubernetes v1.20
mission.description: Successfully finished pods show stuck in pending phase indefinitely in GKE + Kubernetes v1.20. Requested by 85+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo-workflows deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo-workflows version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
helm list -n argo-workflows 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo-workflows installation.

steps.title: Review argo-workflows configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-workflows configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
kubectl get configmap -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-workflows
```
We were running reasonably complex Argo workflows without issues for a long time.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Successfully finished pods show stuck in pending phase
steps.description:
> We have used 's ALWAYS_OFFLOAD_NODE_STATUS workaround and we were able to at least finish our workload without Timeout issues.
> Without it, whole workflow fails around 70/300 step so it's probably connected to the size of the workload being written?
> However with this workaround Argo and
```yaml
# Simplified failing part of workflow. 
 Parents-parent
    our-workflow-1625018400-118234800:
      boundaryID: our-workflow-1625018400-2940179300
      children:
        - our-workflow-1625018400-1820417972
        - our-workflow-1625018400-1463043834
      displayName: '[2]'
      finishedAt: null
      id: our-workflow-1625018400-118234800
      name: our-workflow-1625018400[2].fill-supervised-product-clusters[0].fill-supervised-product-cluster(19:configs:["things"],products:["params"],supervised-configs:[])[2]
      phase: Running
      progress: 0/2
      startedAt: "2021-06-30T09:34:44Z
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
kubectl get events -n argo-workflows --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Successfully finished pods show stuck in pending phase" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
> We have used 's ALWAYS_OFFLOAD_NODE_STATUS workaround and we were able to at least finish our workload without Timeout issues.
> Without it, whole workflow fails around 70/300 step so it's probably connected to the size of the workload being written?
> However with this workaround Argo and Argo-UI doesn't work properly as mentioned in his comment.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-workflows installed or the issue environment reproducible.
